question_text: Describe one method of reducing energy waste in a home and explain how it works.
correct_answer: Accept any one of: (1) Adding insulation to walls/loft/pipes – traps air which is a poor conductor of heat, reducing heat loss through conduction; (2) Using double-glazed windows – the air gap between panes acts as insulation; (3) Draught-proofing doors/windows – prevents warm air escaping; (4) Using thermostats – reduces unnecessary heating; (5) Lagging pipes – reduces heat loss from hot water pipes.
explanation: Any valid method that reduces heat loss or energy waste is acceptable. Students should explain both what the method is and why it works, showing understanding of conduction, insulation, or thermal conductivity concepts.
